title: Diagnostics CLI integration — per-tool config, collector wiring, threshold overrides
purpose: The CLI needs to wire the DiagnosticCollector into LoopContext and ToolContext, configure per-tool validation thresholds from profiles and CLI overrides, and render diagnostics in all three output formats. N-025 adds the LoopContext field and runner push sites in libnorn. This brief wires them from the CLI side: construct the collector, set it on LoopContext, publish it on ToolContext, load tool_config from profiles, apply -c overrides for tool thresholds, and drain/render at step completion.
task: Construct an Arc<DiagnosticCollector> and set it on LoopContext::diagnostics before calling run_agent_step. Publish the same collector on ToolContext via ToolRegistry's shared context (insert_extension). Parse a tool_config section from the profile to configure per-tool thresholds (write.max_code_lines, glob overrides). Support -c write.max_code_lines=N CLI overrides that take precedence over profile values. Construct WriteTool with the resolved LengthLimit (None when no threshold configured). Drain the collector after run_agent_step and render per output format: text mode renders on stderr, json mode includes in the diagnostics envelope field, stream-json mode emits as diagnostic NDJSON events before the completed event.

requirements.title: Construct and wire DiagnosticCollector
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL construct an `Arc<DiagnosticCollector>` at CLI startup (before the first `run_agent_step` call). THE SYSTEM SHALL set it on `LoopContext::diagnostics` as `Some(Arc::clone(&collector))`. THE SYSTEM SHALL publish the same collector on the `ToolRegistry`'s shared `ToolContext` via `insert_extension(Arc::clone(&collector))`. THE SYSTEM SHALL retain a clone of the `Arc` for draining after step completion.
acceptance: LoopContext::diagnostics is Some when run_agent_step is called
ToolContext has a DiagnosticCollector available via get_extension after registry construction
The same collector instance is shared between LoopContext and ToolContext
The CLI can drain the collector after run_agent_step returns

requirements.title: Profile tool_config section for per-tool thresholds
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL parse an optional `tool_config` section from the profile. WHEN `tool_config.write.max_code_lines` is set, THE SYSTEM SHALL construct a `LengthLimit` with `default: Some(value)`. WHEN `tool_config.write.length_overrides` is an array of `{pattern, limit}` objects, THE SYSTEM SHALL add them as glob overrides to the `LengthLimit`. WHEN `tool_config.write` is absent, THE SYSTEM SHALL construct `LengthLimit::none()`. THE SYSTEM SHALL construct `WriteTool::with_length_limit(limit)` using the resolved `LengthLimit`.
acceptance: Profile with tool_config.write.max_code_lines = 500 constructs LengthLimit with default Some(500)
Profile with tool_config.write.length_overrides = [{pattern: 'tests/**', limit: 800}] adds glob override
Profile without tool_config section constructs LengthLimit::none()
WriteTool is constructed with the resolved LengthLimit

requirements.title: CLI -c overrides for tool thresholds
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL recognise `-c write.max_code_lines=N` as a tool-specific config override. WHEN this override is present, THE SYSTEM SHALL set `LengthLimit.default` to `Some(N)`, taking precedence over the profile value. Unknown `-c write.*` keys SHALL produce a warning and be ignored. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT accept `-c` tool overrides for tool names that are not registered.
acceptance: -c write.unknown_key=foo produces a warning and is ignored
Without -c write.max_code_lines, profile value is used; without profile, LengthLimit::none()

requirements.title: Drain and render diagnostics per output format
requirements.spec: AFTER `run_agent_step` returns, THE SYSTEM SHALL drain the `DiagnosticCollector`. IN text mode: non-empty diagnostics SHALL be rendered on stderr with severity, code, message, and suggestion. IN json mode: diagnostics SHALL be included in the output envelope under a `diagnostics` key as an array of objects with severity, code, message, source_tool, file_path, and suggestion fields. IN stream-json mode: diagnostics SHALL be emitted as `{"type":"diagnostic","severity":"...","code":"...","message":"..."}` NDJSON events before the completed event. WHEN diagnostics are empty, no diagnostic output SHALL be produced.
acceptance: Text mode with diagnostics renders them on stderr with severity prefix
Text mode with no diagnostics produces no diagnostic output on stderr
JSON mode includes diagnostics array (empty when none present)
Stream-json mode emits diagnostic events before completed event
Each diagnostic object includes severity, code, and message at minimum

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement RuntimePostValidateCheck for external linters — that is future work
SHALL NOT implement bypass detection — that is a separate libnorn brief
SHALL NOT modify libnorn types — this brief only consumes the LoopContext::diagnostics field added by N-025
SHALL NOT implement REPL-specific diagnostic display — print mode only in this brief
SHALL NOT add diagnostics crate as a dependency of norn-cli — uses only libnorn's NornDiagnostic and DiagnosticCollector
verification: cargo clippy -p norn-cli -- -D warnings produces zero warnings
cargo fmt -p norn-cli -- --check produces no diff
cargo test -p norn-cli passes all tests
No file exceeds 500 lines of code
No code in mod.rs beyond pub mod declarations and re-exports
DiagnosticCollector wiring test verifies it is set on both LoopContext and ToolContext
Profile tool_config test verifies LengthLimit construction from profile values
-c override test verifies CLI override takes precedence over profile
Diagnostic rendering test verifies text, json, and stream-json formats
